q01.title: Retrieval-Augmented Scientific Question Answering
q01.text: Large language models can answer scientific questions fluently, but they may hallucinate claims that are not supported by the source paper. Retrieval-augmented generation reduces this risk by first retrieving relevant passages and then conditioning the answer on that evidence.

q01.title: Graph Representations for Protein Folding
q01.text: Protein structures can be represented as graphs in which amino acid residues are nodes and spatial contacts are edges. Graph neural networks use this representation to learn local and long-range structural patterns.

q01.title: Weak Supervision for Medical Image Segmentation
q01.text: A consistency loss between augmented views improved segmentation masks under weak labels. The method performed best when image-level tags were combined with a small number of bounding boxes.

q02.title: Retrieval-Augmented Scientific Question Answering
q02.text: The system indexes each paper at paragraph level. At query time, it ranks paragraphs with a lexical retriever and a dense embedding retriever, then passes the highest scoring evidence to a downstream answer module.

q02.title: Retrieval-Augmented Scientific Question Answering

q02.title: Weak Supervision for Medical Image Segmentation
q02.text: A consistency loss between augmented views improved segmentation masks under weak labels. The method performed best when image-level tags were combined with a small number of bounding boxes.

q03.title: Solid Electrolytes for Lithium Batteries
q03.text: Solid-state lithium batteries replace flammable liquid electrolytes with ceramic or polymer electrolytes. This design can improve safety, but ion transport across the electrode-electrolyte interface remains a central challenge.

q03.title: Solid Electrolytes for Lithium Batteries
q03.text: Interface coatings lowered impedance after repeated charge cycles. The coated cells retained more capacity because the protective layer reduced side reactions at the lithium metal surface.

q03.title: Uncertainty in Regional Climate Projections
q03.text: The largest uncertainty appeared in summer precipitation, where convective storms were difficult to resolve. Temperature trends were more consistent across models than rainfall extremes.

q05.title: Uncertainty in Regional Climate Projections
q05.text: Regional climate projections combine multiple global climate models with downscaling methods. Ensemble spread is used as a practical estimate of uncertainty when models disagree about precipitation or temperature trends.

q05.title: Graph Representations for Protein Folding
q05.text: Contact-map accuracy improved when the model combined sequence features with geometric edge attributes. The improvement was strongest for long-range residue interactions.

q05.text: The largest uncertainty appeared in summer precipitation, where convective storms were difficult to resolve. Temperature trends were more consistent across models than rainfall extremes.

q09.title: Weak Supervision for Medical Image Segmentation
q09.text: Medical image segmentation usually requires pixel-level annotations from experts, which are expensive to collect. Weak supervision uses cheaper labels such as bounding boxes or image-level tags to reduce annotation cost.
